evidence_quotes.quote: Many companies producing/publishing games today benefit financially from making the games they sell to consumers unplayable over time.
The consumer therefore pays a significant price for a product to which the creator or publisher may suddenly deny access.

paragraphs.text: Many companies producing/publishing games today benefit financially from making the games they sell to consumers unplayable over time. The consumer therefore pays a significant price for a product to which the creator or publisher may suddenly deny access. In addition to the fact that a purchased product is treated as a paid loan, which can be withdrawn when the creator decides, it is also a destruction of a piece of history. I personally find it a very worrying phenomenon and hope that there is scope in this legislation to address it.
